name: Sentinel 1
type: Satellite Radar Data (Synthetic Aperture Radar - SAR)
area: Global coverage, including land and ocean
availability: Free and open access via Copernicus Dataspace Ecosystem
update_frequency: 6-12 days (depending on location and mission phase) - currently 12 days (1 satellite)
legal_restrictions: Open data policy under the Copernicus program
description: Sentinel-1 consists of two satellites (Sentinel-1A & 1B, with 1C planned) providing C-band SAR imagery for applications like land monitoring, maritime surveillance, and disaster response.
variable_s:
Sentinel-1 provides:
  - Backscatter Coefficient (σ⁰): Reflectivity of surfaces (land, water, ice).
  - Coherence: Surface change detection (InSAR).
  - Phase Information: Ground displacement and elevation (InSAR).
  - Polarization (VV, VH, HH, HV): Surface type differentiation. 
  - Doppler Information: Ocean currents and moving object detection.
  - Georeferenced Radar Images: SAR images in various acquisition modes.
spatial_resolution: Varies by mode: 5m (Spotlight), 10m (Interferometric Wide Swath), 25m+ (Stripmap, Extra-Wide Swath)
temporal_resolution: 6-12 days (depending on revisit time and area)
api_protocol:
The Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem catalogue is a web service that can be queried through 4 REST protocols
  - OData
  - STAC
  - OpenSearch
  - Sentinel Hub Catalog API
limitations: Large data volumes requiring significant storage and processing; InSAR analysis requires preprocessing; Noise in certain conditions
